text: added 47 things to my cart at 2am and then just closed the tab like it never happened
my toddler said no to every option then cried because i didnt give him a choice
googled 'headache left side' and now im writing my will
tried to return a shirt i definitely bought in 2019 without a receipt. almost worked
belted out bohemian rhapsody in the shower and honestly the acoustics carried me
someone cut in front of me at the deli and then their number got skipped lol karma
spent 20 min looking for the rental car in a parking garage. it was a white camry. they were all white camrys
i do the work of 3 people and get paid in experience apparently
its 1am and im comparing vacuum cleaners like my life depends on it
bought a whole outfit at 3am that i will never wear in public
left 6 full carts on 6 different websites tonight. im basically an art installation
my 3 year old negotiated an extra cookie by fake crying and i respect the hustle
googled 'why does my knee click' and webmd said i have 3 days to live
the return desk lady recognized me. we're basically coworkers now
sang adele in the shower so good my neighbor clapped. or banged on the wall. same thing
guy who cut the queue got the worst barista. universe provides
pressed every button in the rental car trying to find the headlights. found the trunk release twice
my job title says intern but my responsibilities say ceo
vacuuming at midnight because i saw one crumb and now the whole floor is my enemy
amazon at 2am hits different. just ordered a pasta maker and a kazoo
abandoned my cart because shipping was $4.99 on a $200 order. its the principle
my toddler offered me a bite of his snack then screamed when i took it
i sneezed weird and googled it. apparently i might be a horse
returned something to target that i bought at walmart and nobody stopped me
my shower concert tonight was just me humming because i forgot the lyrics
watched someone skip the line at starbucks and get the wrong order. balance restored
couldnt figure out how to start the rental. it was a push button. im 34
they asked me to train my replacement and my replacement is also an unpaid intern
its 11pm and im reorganizing the pantry. the cereal boxes must be alphabetical
3am me has very different shopping priorities than 9am me
filled a cart with $400 of skincare then remembered i use bar soap
negotiated with a 2 year old for 15 min about wearing pants. he won
googled 'heart fluttering' turns out i just had too much coffee. again
i return things so often the store gave me my own parking spot (in my dreams)
shower acoustics made me think i could audition for something. i cannot
someone budged in line at the post office then got sent to the wrong window 3 times
drove the rental car for 2 days before realizing it was in sport mode
my internship ended 4 years ago but the vibes remain
11:47pm is actually the perfect time to rearrange furniture right
bought a 3am waffle maker. no regrets. ok some regrets
my abandoned carts across the internet could furnish a house
toddler said she wanted milk then threw it at the wall. classic power move
webmd told me my eye twitch is either stress or a brain thing. cool cool cool
returned a candle because it smelled like candle. no receipt needed apparently
i do a full setlist in the shower. opener, encore, the whole thing
the queue skipper at the dmv got the trainee. poetic justice
sat in the rental car for 5 min trying to adjust the mirrors. gave up. drove anyway
doing 3 peoples jobs and they wont even let me expense my lunch
mopping at midnight because the floor looked at me wrong
midnight shopping cart: heated blanket, 4 books ill never read, novelty socks
added everything to wishlist instead of cart. growth
my toddler asked for water then said 'not that water' what other water is there
googled 'chest pain left side' at 4am and made peace with god over nothing
brought back jeans from 2 years ago with the tags still on. they took them
shower thought: if i sang this good outside the bathroom id be famous
line cutter at the grocery store picked the lane that broke down lmaooo
the rental car beeped at me 47 times and i still dont know why
my boss said exposure is payment. my landlord disagrees
deep cleaning the bathroom at 1am because i cant sleep and dirt is personal
impulsively bought a star projector at midnight. my ceiling looks amazing tho
i have 23 abandoned carts. each one is a life i couldve lived
offered my kid apple or banana. she said pizza. shes 2. shes right tho
my ankle cracked and i googled it and now i think i need surgery
returned a blender with smoothie still in it once. we dont talk about it
sometimes i just stand in the shower singing to the shampoo bottles. they get me
saw someone jump the queue at the pharmacy and then their prescription wasnt ready
locked myself out of the rental car in a wendys parking lot
if being an unpaid intern was a sport id have a gold medal
running the dishwasher at midnight like a responsible chaotic adult
2am purchase: a 6ft inflatable dinosaur. he arrives thursday
put 14 things in cart. shipping estimate: 2 weeks. closed tab immediately
my toddler said 'i do it myself' and then stared at me until i did it
googled 'weird mole' and now im convinced im in a medical drama
returned shoes i wore to a wedding. sorry not sorry
the shower is the only venue where im a grammy winner
line skipper at airport security got randomly selected for extra screening lol
tried to use the windshield wipers in the rental and turned on the blinker for 8 miles
they promoted me to senior unpaid intern. still no benefits
organizing my spice rack at 12:30am because cumin was behind the paprika and thats chaos
bought a hammock at 4am. i live in a studio apartment
saved my cart for later and later never came. rip those shoes
my kid negotiated bedtime from 7:30 to 8:15. she'll make a great lawyer
arm went numb because i slept on it. googled it anyway. regret
return policy says 30 days. i say 30 is a mindset
sang so loud in the shower the dog howled along. duet of the century
someone jumped the queue at chipotle and they forgot his guac. the universe remembers
accidentally put the rental in reverse in a drive thru. we dont speak of it
my whole career is just one long unpaid trial period
started vacuuming at 11:55pm. neighbors love me probably
3am impulse buy: a bread maker. i dont even eat bread that much
had $300 in my cart and then thought 'do i need this' and the answer was no but also yes
told my toddler we're leaving the park in 5 min. that was 40 min ago
googled 'why is my eye red' at 2am. turns out i was just tired. from googling
returned a pillow because it was too soft. they asked no questions. legends
my shower concert got a standing ovation from the bathmat
cut the line at the deli once. got the wrong meat. i deserved it honestly
spent 10 min finding the gas cap on the rental. its on the other side. always
being an adult is just being an unpaid intern for your own life
folding laundry at midnight while watching true crime. peak adulting
midnight purchase update: the kazoo arrived and it slaps
